Course Title: AI in Clinical Trials: Bias and Validity
Category: Healthcare & Life Sciences: Medical Services, Pharmacy, Biotechnology, Hospitals
Who is this for: Clinical research coordinators, clinical data managers, regulatory affairs specialists, biostatisticians, medical affairs professionals, clinical trial managers, research nurses, clinical research associates
Team or Dept this is for: Clinical Research, Medical Affairs, Regulatory Affairs, Quality Assurance, Data Management, Biostatistics
Description: Risks in patient selection, analysis, and reporting.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with clinical trial concepts (such as phases, endpoints, and patient recruitment) and an openness to learning about artificial intelligence applications in healthcare are all that is required to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify common sources of bias in AI-driven clinical trials, including risks in patient selection, data analysis, and results reporting. You will also be able to explain how these biases affect the validity of trial outcomes and apply practical strategies to recognise and reduce their impact in real-world healthcare and life sciences settings.
